sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 13.10 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.13)

statement.latex: Is there a function$f\! : { N} { N}$such that, for every soluble group$G$of derived length$k$generated by a set$A$, the validity of the identity$x^4=1$on each subgroup generated by at most$f(k)$elements of$A$implies that$G$is a group of exponent 4? { V.\,V.\,Bludov} No, there is not (G.\,S.\,Deryabina, A.\,N.\,Krasil'nikov, Siberian Math. J., { 44}, no.\,1 (2003), 58--60).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
